Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\n# Simple LRU cache for router classifications (reduces redundant LLM calls)\nclass RouterCache:\n    \"\"\"LRU cache for intent classification results.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, max_size: int = 100):\n        self.cache: OrderedDict = OrderedDict()\n        self.max_size = max_size\n        self.hits = 0\n        self.misses = 0\n\n    def _make_key(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create cache key from utterance and session context.\"\"\"\n        # Normalize utterance for cache key (lowercase, strip extra whitespace)\n        normalized = \" \".join(utterance.lower().split())\n        return f\"{session_id}:{normalized}\"\n\n    def get(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> Optional[list[IntentClassification]]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached classification if available.\"\"\"\n        key = self._make_key(utterance, session_id)\n        if key in self.cache:\n            # Move to end (most recently used)\n            self.cache.move_to_end(key)\n            self.hits += 1\n            return self.cache[key]\n        self.misses += 1\n        return None\n\n    def put(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n        key = self._make_key(utterance, session_id)\n        self.cache[key] = classifications\n        self.cache.move_to_end(key)\n\n        # Evict oldest if over capacity\n        if len(self.cache) > self.max_size:\n            self.cache.popitem(last=False)\n\n    def invalidate(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate all cache entries for a session.\"\"\"\n        keys_to_delete = [k for k in self.cache if k.startswith(f\"{session_id}:\")]\n        for key in keys_to_delete:\n            del self.cache[key]\n\n    def get_stats(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get cache statistics.\"\"\"\n        total = self.hits + self.misses\n        hit_rate = self.hits / total if total > 0 else 0\n        return {\n            \"size\": len(self.cache),\n            \"hits\": self.hits,\n            \"misses\": self.misses,\n            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n        }\n\n\n# Global cache instance\n_router_cache: Optional[RouterCache] = None\n\n\ndef get_router_cache() -> RouterCache:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global router cache instance.\"\"\"\n    global _router_cache\n    if _router_cache is None:\n        _router_cache = RouterCache(max_size=100)\n    return _router_cache\n\n\n# Simple heuristic patterns for fast-path routing\nclass FastPathRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fast-path heuristic router for common patterns.\n\n    Bypasses LLM for straightforward utterances, reducing latency from ~2s to ~5ms.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Common project slug patterns\n    PROJECT_PATTERNS = {\n        \"pbx\": [\"pbx\", \"pbx-web\", \"pbx web\", \"phone system\"],\n        \"whisper\": [\"whisper\", \"whisper-stt\", \"whisper stt\", \"speech to text\", \"stt\"],\n        \"pipeline\": [\"pipeline\", \"options-pipeline\", \"kalshi pipeline\"],\n        \"domain\": [\"domain\", \"domain-check\", \"domain check\"],\n        \"mta\": [\"mta\", \"mta-my-way\", \"subway\"],\n        \"armor\": [\"armor\", \"armord\"],\n        \"vista\": [\"vista\"],\n        \"clasp\": [\"clasp\"],\n        \"sigil\": [\"sigil\"],\n        \"forge\": [\"forge\"],\n        \"needle\": [\"needle\", \"bead\", \"beads\", \"br\"],\n        \"agent\": [\"agent\", \"bot\", \"agents\"],\n        \"duck\": [\"duck\", \"duck-e\", \"duc\"],\n        \"claud\": [\"claude\", \"claude-code\"],\n        \"spaxel\": [\"spaxel\"],\n        \"kalshi\": [\"kalshi\", \"kalshi-tape\", \"kalshi weather\"],\n        \"ardenone\": [\"ardenone\", \"ardenone-cluster\", \"cluster\"],\n        \"apexalgo\": [\"apexalgo\", \"apexalgo-iad\"],\n        \"rs-manager\": [\"rs-manager\"],\n    }\n\n    # Intent type patterns\n    INTENT_PATTERNS = {\n        IntentType.STATUS: [\n            \"status\", \"state\", \"current\", \"running\", \"healthy\", \"up\", \"down\",\n            \"what's\", \"what is\", \"how\", \"check\", \"show\", \"get\", \"list\",\n            \"are the\", \"is the\", \"do we have\", \"can you see\", \"tell me\"\n        ],\n        IntentType.LOOKUP: [\n            \"logs\", \"log output\", \"error\", \"find\", \"search\", \"lookup\", \"config\",\n            \"configuration\", \"setting\", \"where\", \"pull up\", \"show me\", \"get the\",\n            \"recent\", \"latest\", \"output\", \"trace\"\n        ],\n        IntentType.BRAINSTORM: [\n            \"brainstorm\", \"explore\", \"think about\", \"ideas\", \"options\", \"suggest\",\n            \"improvements\", \"design\", \"architecture\", \"consider\", \"what if\"\n        ],\n        IntentType.ACTION: [\n            \"deploy\", \"restart\", \"create\", \"delete\", \"update\", \"apply\", \"run\",\n            \"execute\", \"start\", \"stop\", \"scale\", \"build\", \"install\"\n        ],\n    }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def try_fast_path(\n        cls,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Attempt fast-path classification using heuristics.\n\n        Returns None if fast-path fails, falling back to LLM.\n        \"\"\"\n        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n\n        # Fast-path only works for simple, clear utterances\n        # Skip if:\n        # - Multiple sentences (likely multi-intent)\n        # - Too long (>200 chars)\n        # - Contains ambiguous phrases\n        if len(utterance) > 200:\n            return None\n\n        if \".\" in utterance and utterance.count(\".\") > 1:\n            return None\n\n        # Detect multi-intent patterns\n        multi_intent_markers = [\" and \", \" & \", \" plus \", \" also \", \" as well as \"]\n        for marker in multi_intent_markers:\n            if marker in utterance_lower:\n                return None  # Multi-intent - let LLM handle it\n\n        # Detect project slug\n        project_slug = None\n        for slug, patterns in cls.PROJECT_PATTERNS.items():\n            for pattern in patterns:\n                if pattern in utterance_lower:\n                    project_slug = slug\n                    break\n            if project_slug:\n                break\n\n        # Detect intent type\n        intent_type = None\n        lookup_kind = None\n\n        for intent, patterns in cls.INTENT_PATTERNS.items():\n            for pattern in patterns:\n                if pattern in utterance_lower:\n                    intent_type = intent\n\n                    # Special handling for lookup intents\n                    if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP:\n                        if \"log\" in utterance_lower:\n                            lookup_kind = \"logs\"\n                        elif \"config\" in utterance_lower or \"setting\" in utterance_lower:\n                            lookup_kind = \"config\"\n                        else:\n                            lookup_kind = \"docs\"\n                    break\n            if intent_type:\n                break\n\n        # Default to STATUS if no clear intent detected\n        if intent_type is None:\n            intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n        # Create classification\n        classification = IntentClassification(\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            confidence=0.85,  # Lower confidence for heuristic\n            utterance_fragment=utterance,\n            reasoning=\"Fast-path heuristic classification\",\n            urgency=\"normal\",\n            lookup_kind=lookup_kind,\n        )\n\n        return [classification]\n\n\ndef get_fast_path_router() -> FastPathRouter:\n    \"\"\"Get the fast-path router instance (singleton).\"\"\"\n    return FastPathRouter\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n        self._system_prompt_cache: str | None = None\n        self._system_prompt_mtime: float = 0\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client with optimized timeout for routing.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            # Use shorter timeout for routing (8s instead of 30s default)\n            # Router classification should be fast with Haiku model\n            # Reduced from 10s to meet ~500ms budget more aggressively\n            from ..escalate.llm import ZAIClient\n            self._zai_client = ZAIClient(timeout=8.0)\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self, force_reload: bool = False) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\n\n        Uses cache to avoid disk I/O on every call. Only reloads when the prompt\n        file changes (based on mtime) or when force_reload=True.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if we need to reload (file modified or cache empty)\n        try:\n            current_mtime = self.prompt_path.stat().st_mtime\n            if not force_reload and self._system_prompt_cache is not None and current_mtime == self._system_prompt_mtime:\n                # Cache is valid, return cached prompt\n                return self._system_prompt_cache\n        except OSError:\n            # File doesn't exist or can't be accessed\n            pass\n\n        # Need to rebuild the prompt\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n\n        # Update cache\n        self._system_prompt_cache = system_prompt\n        try:\n            self._system_prompt_mtime = self.prompt_path.stat().st_mtime\n        except OSError:\n            self._system_prompt_mtime = 0\n\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n        use_cache: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n            use_cache: If True, check cache before LLM call\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n\n        # Check cache first (unless disabled)\n        if use_cache:\n            cache = get_router_cache()\n            cached_result = cache.get(utterance, session_id)\n            if cached_result is not None:\n                cache_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache hit for session {session_id} ({cache_ms}ms)\")\n                return cached_result\n\n        # Try fast-path heuristic routing (bypasses LLM for common patterns)\n        fast_path_start = time.monotonic()\n        fast_path_result = FastPathRouter.try_fast_path(utterance, session_id)\n        if fast_path_result is not None:\n            fast_path_ms = int((time.monotonic() - fast_path_start) * 1000)\n            logger.info(f\"Fast-path routing succeeded for session {session_id} ({fast_path_ms}ms)\")\n            # Cache the fast-path result\n            if use_cache:\n                cache = get_router_cache()\n                cache.put(utterance, session_id, fast_path_result)\n            return fast_path_result\n\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        # IMPORTANT: Session context fetch is now skipped entirely for latency.\n        # Recent intent context provides minimal routing value and costs 2-10ms per call.\n        # The prompt already includes project patterns and intent definitions.\n        # Re-enable only if routing accuracy degrades in production.\n        session_context = \"\"  # Disabled for latency optimization (adc-25sn9)\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            llm_start = time.monotonic()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # Use Haiku for faster routing (optimized for classification)\n                max_tokens=256,  # Reduced from 512 - router outputs are small JSON arrays; 256 is sufficient\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n            llm_ms = int((time.monotonic() - llm_start) * 1000)\n            logger.info(f\"Router LLM call took {llm_ms}ms for session {session_id}\")\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n\n            total_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n            logger.info(f\"Router classify_utterance total: {total_ms}ms (LLM: {llm_ms}ms)\")\n\n            # Cache the result for future calls\n            if use_cache:\n                cache = get_router_cache()\n                cache.put(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                except json.JSONDecode as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(e),\n                        raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_response or \"\",\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n            }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n Returns ``{q: value}``. Raises\n96\t    ``ValueError`` on an empty list — callers should skip empty stages rather\n97\t    than call this (the store does so).\n98\t    \"\"\"\n99\t    if not values:\n100\t        raise ValueError(\"percentiles() requires at least one value\")\n101\t    ordered = sorted(values)\n102\t    n = len(ordered)\n103\t    out: dict[int, int] = {}\n104\t    for q in qs:\n105\t        if not 0 <= q <= 100:\n106\t            raise ValueError(f\"percentile q must be in [0, 100], got {q}\")\n107\t        idx = (q * n + 99) // 100 - 1  # ceil(q/100 * n) - 1\n108\t        idx = max(0, min(n - 1, idx))\n109\t        out[q] = ordered[idx]\n110\t    return out\n111\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fea117c0-38f6-4aa4-af82-f01cb33eff82","uuid":"0c95ee68-ffd0-4739-8f29-489a8f63a651","timestamp":"2026-07-24T02:11:56.357Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/instrument/timings.py","content":"\"\"\"Per-stage timing capture helpers for dispatch instrumentation.\n\n``DispatchTimings`` is a small accumulator that records stage durations in\nmilliseconds during a single dispatch. It is passed through the router\n(``route_utterance`` → ``process_intent`` → the fetch/synthesize/escalate\nbranches); each stage records the duration it measures, and ``process_intent``\npersists one ``dispatch_timings`` row per intent thread via\n``SessionStore.record_dispatch_timings(**timings.to_fields())``.\n\nDesign notes:\n\n- The clock is injectable (default ``time.monotonic``) so tests get\n  deterministic durations without sleeping.\n- Only stages actually measured appear in ``to_fields()``; unmeasured stages\n  are simply absent (the store upsert leaves them NULL), which is the honest\n  representation — e.g. ``synthesize_first_token_ms`` cannot be measured until\n  the synthesize strand streams, and ``escalate_ms`` is absent for hot-path\n  dispatches.\n- ``percentiles`` uses the nearest-rank method (ceil(q/100 * n)), matching how\n  latency SLOs are typically reported. SQLite has no built-in percentile, so\n  the store fetches ordered per-stage values and calls into here.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport time\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\n\n\nClock = Callable[[], float]\n\n# The timing stages tracked per dispatch. Mirrors the dispatch_timings columns\n# (minus intent_id/created_at). Kept here as the canonical list the collector\n# accepts so a typo in a stage name is a KeyError, not a silent no-op.\nDISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES = (\n    \"router_ms\",\n    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n    \"escalate_ms\",\n    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n    \"stt_ms\",\n    \"first_render_ms\",\n)\n\n\nclass DispatchTimings:\n    \"\"\"Accumulates per-stage millisecond durations for one dispatch.\n\n    Usage in the router::\n\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        fetch_start = timings.clock()\n        ...\n        timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, clock: Clock = time.monotonic):\n        self._clock = clock\n        self._durations: dict[str, int] = {}\n\n    @property\n    def clock(self) -> Clock:\n        \"\"\"The monotonic clock callable (exposed so callers share one clock).\"\"\"\n        return self._clock\n\n    def record(self, stage: str, ms: int | float | None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record a stage duration in ms. None is ignored (stage stays absent).\"\"\"\n        if ms is None:\n            return\n        if stage not in DISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES:\n            raise KeyError(f\"Unknown dispatch timing stage: {stage!r}\")\n        self._durations[stage] = int(ms)\n\n    def elapsed_ms(self, start: float, end: float | None = None) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Milliseconds between ``start`` and ``end`` (default: now), via the clock.\"\"\"\n        return int(((end if end is not None else self._clock()) - start) * 1000)\n\n    def to_fields(self) -> dict[str, int]:\n        \"\"\"Return the recorded stages as a kwargs dict for record_dispatch_timings.\"\"\"\n        return dict(self._durations)\n\n    def get(self, stage: str) -> Optional[int]:\n        \"\"\"Return a recorded stage duration, or None if unmeasured.\"\"\"\n        return self._durations.get(stage)\n\n\ndef percentiles(values: list[int], qs: tuple[int, ...] = (50, 95)) -> dict[int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Nearest-rank percentiles for a list of integer samples.\n\n    Each ``q`` (0..100) maps to ``values[ceil(q/100 * n) - 1]`` after sorting\n    ascending, clamped to ``[0, n-1]``. Returns ``{q: value}``. Raises\n    ``ValueError`` on an empty list — callers should skip empty stages rather\n    than call this (the store does so).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not values:\n        raise ValueError(\"percentiles() requires at least one value\")\n    ordered = sorted(values)\n    n = len(ordered)\n    out: dict[int, int] = {}\n    for q in qs:\n        if not 0 <= q <= 100:\n            raise ValueError(f\"percentile q must be in [0, 100], got {q}\")\n        idx = (q * n + 99) // 100 - 1  # ceil(q/100 * n) - 1\n        idx = max(0, min(n - 1, idx))\n        out[q] = ordered[idx]\n    return out\n","numLines":111,"startLine":1,"totalLines":111}}}

start) * 1000\n    print(f\"  Utterance: {utterance1}\")\n    print(f\"  Time: {int(elapsed1)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Intents: {len(result1)}\")\n    print(f\"  Type: {result1[0].intent_type.value if result1 else 'none'}\")\n\n    # Test 2: Cache hit (same utterance)\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"-\" * 60)\n    print(\"Test 2: Same utterance (cache hit)\")\n    start = time.monotonic()\n    result2 = await router.classify_utterance(utterance1, session_id)\n    elapsed2 = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n    print(f\"  Time: {int(elapsed2)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Cache hit: {elapsed2 < 10}\")\n\n    # Test 3: Cache miss with multi-intent (requires LLM)\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"-\" * 60)\n    print(\"Test 3: Multi-intent utterance (LLM call, cache miss)\")\n    utterance3 = \"has the pbx web caught up and what's the state of whisper stt\"\n    cache.invalidate(session_id)  # Clear cache to force LLM call\n    start = time.monotonic()\n    result3 = await router.classify_utterance(utterance3, session_id)\n    elapsed3 = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n    print(f\"  Utterance: {utterance3}\")\n    print(f\"  Time: {int(elapsed3)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Intents: {len(result3)}\")\n    for i, r in enumerate(result3):\n        print(f\"    Intent {i+1}: {r.intent_type.value} → {r.project_slug or 'no project'}\")\n\n    # Test 4: Multiple runs of multi-intent (warming cache)\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"-\" * 60)\n    print(\"Test 4: Multi-intent repeated (3 runs, cache populated)\")\n    times = []\n    for i in range(3):\n        start = time.monotonic()\n        result = await router.classify_utterance(utterance3, session_id)\n        elapsed = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n        times.append(elapsed)\n        print(f\"  Run {i+1}: {int(elapsed)}ms ({len(result)} intents)\")\n\n    p50 = int(sorted(times)[1])\n    p95 = int(sorted(times)[2])\n    print(f\"  p50: {p50}ms | p95: {p95}ms\")\n\n    # Test 5: Session context fetch measurement\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"-\" * 60)\n    print(\"Test 5: Session context fetch latency\")\n    cache.invalidate(session_id + \"-ctx\")\n    start = time.monotonic()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    ctx_time = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n    print(f\"  Session fetch: {int(ctx_time)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Session exists: {session is not None}\")\n\n    start = time.monotonic()\n    intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=3)\n    intents_time = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n    print(f\"  Recent intents fetch: {int(intents_time)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Intents count: {len(intents)}\")\n    print(f\"  Total session context: {int(ctx_time + intents_time)}ms\")\n\n    # Summary\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n    print(\"Summary:\")\n    print(f\"  Fast-path (first call): {int(elapsed1)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Cache hit: {int(elapsed2)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Multi-intent LLM call: {int(elapsed3)}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Multi-intent cached (p50): {p50}ms\")\n    print(f\"  Session context overhead: {int(ctx_time + intents_time)}ms\")\n\n    # Analysis\n    print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n    print(\"Analysis:\")\n    if elapsed2 > 10:\n        print(f\"  ⚠️  Cache hit took {int(elapsed2)}ms - may indicate unnecessary work\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"  ✅ Cache hit is fast ({int(elapsed2)}ms)\")\n\n    if elapsed3 > 2000:\n        print(f\"  ⚠️  Multi-intent LLM call took {int(elapsed3)}ms - exceeds budget\")\n    else:\n        print(f\"  ✅ Multi-intent LLM call is within budget\")\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    asyncio.run(test_router_detailed())\n","numLines":122,"startLine":1,"totalLines":122}}}

results = []\n\n        for utterance, description, expected_path in test_cases:\n            print(f\"\\n{description}\")\n            print(f\"  Utterance: {utterance[:70]}\")\n\n            # Clear (95,))[95])\n            avg = int(statistics.mean(times))\n            print(f\"\\nZAI Call Statistics:\")\n            print(f\"  Average: {avg}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p50: {p50}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p95: {p95}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Min: {int(min(times))}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Max: {int(max(times))}ms\")\n\n            self.results[\"zai_latency\"] = {\n                \"avg_ms\": avg,\n                \"p50_ms\": p50,\n                \"p95_ms\": p95,\n                \"min_ms\": int(min(times)),\n                \"max_ms\": int(max(times)),\n [{i+1}] {int(elapsed)}ms {'(hit)' if is_hit else '(miss)'} \"\n                  f\"- {utterance[:50]}\")\n\n        stats = self.cache.get_stats()\n        hit_rate = stats.get(\"hit_rate\", 0)\n\n        print(f\"\\nCache Statistics:\")\n        print(f\"  Total requests: {len(utterances)}\")\n        print(f\"  Cache hits: {hit_count}\")\n        print(f\"  Cache misses: {miss_count}\")\n             r[\"first_call_ms\"] for r in cls_results\n                if r[\"expected_path\"] == \"fast-path\"\n            ])\n            llm_avg = statistics.mean([\n await self.router.classify_utterance(\n                utterance, session_id, use_cache=True\n            )\n            first_call_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure cache hit\n            start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications_cached = await self.router.classify_utterance(\n                utterance, session_id, use_cache=True\n            )\n            cache_hit_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure with cache disabled (always LLM/fast-path)\n            self.cache.invalidate(session_id)\n            start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications_no_cache = await self.router.classify_utterance(\n                utterance, session_id, use_cache=False\n            )\n            no_cache_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n\n            print(f\"  First call (cache miss): {int(first_call_ms)}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Cache hit: {int(cache_hit_ms)}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Cache {cls.intent_type.value} → {cls.project_slug or 'no project'} \"\n                      f\"(confidence: {cls.confidence})\")\n\n            results.append({\n                \"description\": description,\n                \"utterance\": utterance,\n                \"expected_path\": expected_path,\n                \"first_call_ms\": first_call_ms,\n                \"cache_hit_ms\": cache_hit_ms,\n                \"no_cache_ms\": no_cache_ms,\n                \"intent_count\": len(classifications),\n            })\n\n        self.results[\"classification\"] = results\n        return results\n\n    async def profile_zai_latency(self):\n        \"\"\"Profile ZAI proxy call latency in isolation.\"\"\"\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n        print(\"ZAI PROXY LATENCY PROFILE\")\n        print(\"=\" * 70)\n\n        session_id = \"profile-session-zai\"\n        self.cache.invalidate(session_id)\n\n        # Use a multi-intent utterance to force LLM call\n for i in range(5):\n            self.cache.invalidate(session_id)  # Force cache miss\n            start = time.monotonic()\n\n            # The classify_utterance includes ZAI call time\n            result = await self.router.classify_utterance(\n                utterance, session_id, use_cache=False\n            )\n\n            elapsed = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n            times.append(elapsed)\n            print(f\"  Run {i+1}: {int(elapsed)}ms\")\n\n        if times:\n            p50 = int(statistics.median(times))\n            p95 = int(percentiles(times, (95,))[95])\n            avg = int(statistics.mean(times))\n            print(f\"\\nZAI Call Statistics:\")\n            print(f\"  Average: {avg}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p50: {p50}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p95: {p95}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Min: {int(min(times))}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Max: {int(max(times))}ms\")\n\n            self.results[\"zai_latency\"] = {\n                \"avg_ms\": avg,\n                \"p50_ms\": p50,\n                \"p95_ms\": p95,\n                \"min_ms\": int(min(times)),\n                \"max_ms\": int(max(times)),\n                \"samples\": times,\n            }\n\n        return self.results.get(\"zai_latency\", {})\n\n    async def profile_cache_effectiveness(self):\n        \"\"\"Profile cache hit rates and self.results[\"cache_effectiveness\"] = {\n            \"total_requests\": len(utterances),\n            \"hits\": hit_count,\n            \"misses\": miss_count,\n            \"hit_rate\": (95,))[95])\n            avg = int(statistics.mean(times))\n            print(f\"\\nZAI Call Statistics:\")\n            print(f\"  Average: {avg}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p50: {p50}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p95: {p95}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Min: {int(min(times))}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Max: {int(max(times))}ms\")\n\n            self.results[\"zai_latency\"] = {\n                \"avg_ms\": avg,\n                \"p50_ms\": p50,\n                \"p95_ms\": p95,\n                \"min_ms\": int(min(times)),\n                \"max_ms\": int(max(times)),\n session_id, use_cache=True\n            )\n            elapsed = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n            total_time += elapsed\n\n            # Check if it was a cache hit (fast response)\n            is_hit = elapsed < 50  # Cache hits are typically < 50ms\n            if is_hit:\n                hit_count += 1\n            else:\n                miss_count += 1\n\n            print(f\"  [{i+1}] {int(elapsed)}ms {'(hit)' if is_hit else '(miss)'} \"\n                  f\"- {utterance[:50]}\")\n\n        stats = self.cache.get_stats()\n        hit_rate = stats.get(\"hit_rate\", 0)\n\n        print(f\"\\nCache Statistics:\")\n        print(f\"  Total requests: {len(utterances)}\")\n        print(f\"  Cache hits: {hit_count}\")\n        print(f\"  Cache misses: {miss_count}\")\n        print(f\"  Hit rate: {hit_rate:.1%}\")\n        print(f\"  Total time: {int(total_time)}ms\")\n        print(f\"  Avg per request: {int(total_time / len(utterances))}ms\")\n\n        self.results[\"cache_effectiveness\"] = {\n            \"total_requests\": len(utterances),\n            \"hits\": hit_count,\n            \"misses\": miss_count,\n            \"hit_rate\": ZAI proxy call latency in isolation.\"\"\"\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n        print(\"ZAI PROXY LATENCY PROFILE\")\n        print(\"=\" * 70)\n\n        session_id = utterance, session_id, use_cache=False\n            )\n\n            elapsed = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n            times.append(elapsed)\n            print(f\"  Run {i+1}: {int(elapsed)}ms\")\n\n        if times:\n            p50 = int(statistics.median(times))\n            p95 = int(percentiles(times, (95,))[95])\n            avg = int(statistics.mean(times))\n            print(f\"\\nZAI Call Statistics:\")\n            print(f\"  Average: {avg}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p50: {p50}ms\")\n            print(f\"  p95: {p95}ms\")\n            print(f\"  Min:         \"p95_ms\": p95,\n                \"min_ms\": int(min(times)),\n                \"max_ms\": int(max(times)),\n                \"samples\": times,\n            }\n\n        return self.results.get(\"zai_latency\", {})\n\n    async def profile_cache_effectiveness(self):\n        \"\"\"Profile cache hit rates and effectiveness.\"\"\"\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n        print(\"CACHE EFFECTIVENESS PROFILE\")\n        print(\"=\" * 70)\n\n        session_id = \"profile-session-cache\"\n\n        # Simulate realistic usage pattern\n        utterances = [\n            \"what's the status of pbx\",  # Repeated 3x\n            \"what's the status of pbx\",\n            \"what's the status of pbx\",\n            \"pull up whisper logs\",  # Repeated 2x\n            \"pull up whisper logs\",\n            \"check armor config\",  # Unique\n            \"what's the status of pbx\",  # Again\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"\\nSimulating {len(utterances)} utterances with repetition pattern...\")\n        self.cache.invalidate(session_id)\n\n        total_time = 0\n        hit_count = 0\n        miss_count = 0\n\n        for i, utterance in    elapsed = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n            total_time += elapsed\n\n            # Check if it was a cache hit (fast response)\n            is_hit = elapsed < 50  # Cache hits are typically < 50ms\n            if is_hit:\n                hit_count += 1\n            else:\n                miss_count += 1\n\n            print(f\"  [{i+1}] {int(elapsed)}ms {'(hit)' if is_hit else '(miss)'} \"\n                  f\"- {utterance[:50]}\")\n\n        stats = self.cache.get_stats()\n        hit_rate = stats.get(\"hit_rate\", 0)\n\n        print(f\"\\nCache Statistics:\")\n        print(f\"  Total requests: {len(utterances)}\")\n        print(f\"  Cache hits: {hit_count}\")\n        print(f\"  Cache misses: {miss_count}\")\n        print(f\"  Hit rate: {hit_rate:.1%}\")\n        print(f\"  Total time: {int(total_time)}ms\")\n        print(f\"  Avg per request: {int(total_time / len(utterances))}ms\")\n\n        self.results[\"cache_effectiveness\"] = {\n            \"total_requests\": len(utterances),\n            \"hits\": hit_count,\n            \"misses\": miss_count,\n            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n            \"total_time_ms\": total_time,\n         utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            # Measure route_utterance\n            start = time.monotonic()\n            try:\n                routed_intents = await self.router.route_utterance(\n                    utterance=utterance,\n                    utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                )\n                elapsed = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000\n\n                print(f\"  Time: {int(elapsed)}ms\")\n                print(f\"  Routed intents: {len(routed_intents)}\")\n\n                for routed precision.\"\"\"\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n        print(\"HIGH-RESOLUTION ROUTER CLASSIFICATION PROFILE\")\n        print(\"=\" * 70)\n\n        session_id = \"profile-session-hires\"\n        self.cache.invalidate(session_id)\n\n        # Test cases with different paths\n        test_cases = [\n            (\"Fast-path single intent\", \"what's the status of pbx\"),\n            (\"Fast-path lookup\", \"pull up recent logs for whisper\"),\n            (\"Fast-path brainstorm\", \"brainstorm improvements to armor\"),\n            (\"Multi-intent LLM\", \"check pbx status and {utterance[:70]}\")\n\n            # Warm up\n            self.cache.invalidate(session_id)\n            await self.router.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n            # Measure multiple = await self.router.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n                elapsed = self.time_ms() - start\n                times.append(elapsed)\n\n            # Calculate statistics\n {len(result)}\")\n\n            for i, cls in enumerate(result):\n                print(f\"    [{i+1}] {cls.intent_type.value} → {cls.project_slug or 'no project'}\")\n\n            results.append({\n                \"description\": description,\n                \"utterance\": utterance,\n                \"avg_ms\": avg,\n                \"median_ms\": median,\n                \"p95_ms\": p95,\n                \"min_ms\": min_val,\n                \"max_ms\": max_val,\n                \"intent_count\": len(result),\n                \"samples\": times,\n            })\n\n        self.results[\"router_classification\"] = results\n        return results\n\n    async def profile_cache_vs_nocache(self):\n        \"\"\"Profile cache effectiveness with detailed comparison.\"\"\"\n        print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n        print(\"CACHE VS NO-CACHE COMPARISON\")\n        print(\"=\" * 70)\n\n        session_id = \"profile-session-cache-detail\"\n        utterance = \"what's the status of pbx\"\n\n        print(f\"\\nUtterance: {utterance}\")\n        print(\"\\nMeasuring cache hit vs miss latency...\")\n\n        # Cache miss (classification + cache insert)\n        cache_miss_times = []\n        for _ in range(5):\n            self.cache.invalidate(session_id)\n            start = self.time_ms()\n            await self.router.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id, use_cache=True)\n            elapsed = self.time_ms() - start\n            cache_miss_times.append(elapsed)\n\n        # Cache hit (lookup only)\n        cache_hit_times = []\n        for _ in range(20):\n            start = self.time_ms()\n            await self.router.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id, use_cache=True)\n            elapsed = self.time_ms() - start\n            cache_hit_times.append(elapsed)\n\n        miss_avg = statistics.mean(cache_miss_times)\n        hit_avg = statistics.mean(cache_hit_times)\n        speedup = miss_avg / hit_avg if hit_avg > 0 else 0\n\n        print(f\"\\nCache Miss (5 runs):\")\n        print(f\"  Avg: {miss_avg:.2f}ms | Median: {statistics.median(cache_miss_times):.2f}ms\")\n        print(f\"  p95: {percentiles(cache_miss_times, (95,))[95]:.2f}ms\")\n\n        print(f\"\\nCache Hit (20 runs):\")\n        print(f\"  Avg: {hit_avg:.2f}ms | Median: {statistics.median(cache_hit_times):.2f}ms\")\n        print(f\"  p95: {percentiles(cache_hit_times, (95,))[95]:.2f}ms\")\n\n        print(f\"\\nCache Performance:\")\n        print(f\"  Speedup: {speedup:.1f}x\")\n        print(f\"  Time saved per hit: {miss_avg - hit_avg:.2f}ms\")\n\n        self.results[\"cache_comparison\"] = {\n            \"cache_miss_avg_ms\": miss_avg,\n            \"cache_hit_avg_ms\": hit_avg,\n            \"speedup_x\": speedup,\n            \"time_saved_ms\": miss_avg - hit_avg,\n            \"cache_miss_samples\": cache_miss_times,\n            \"cache_hit_samples\": cache_hit_times,\n        }\n\n
